title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Execution Trace: Concurrency Budget Semaphore
summary: Concurrent handlers race on a map, launch unbounded goroutines, queue work after clients leave, share one global rate limit, and accept more expensive work than dependencies can sustain. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo concurrency budget semaphore increment in concurrency budget and overload decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Concurrency Budget Semaphore · Theory Concurrency Budget Semaphore
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4224 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
